input:
In reply, the Prophet said: “I have heard nothing.” It is simply not known whether Abū Ṭālib was a Muslim when he died. The Prophet was very sad to lose his uncle. Abū Ṭālib was the man who brought up Muhammad, the orphan who had lost both his parents and his grandfather by the time he was eight years old. He took him into his family and treated him like his own son, giving him extra kindness out of sympathy for the bereaved child.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 12: Difficulties in Abundance

output.scene_title: The Prophet Mourns the Loss of Abñ Ábil
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 12: Difficulties in Abundance

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 30s, lean build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a brown cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by brown cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Abñ Ábil
characters.appearance: Late 50s, dark olive skin, sturdy build, slightly taller than average. Thick grey-streaked beard with henna tint, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain black woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Kind gentle eyes, slight smile.

output.moral_lesson: Family is not just by blood, but also by love and compassion. The Prophet's uncle, Abñ Ábil, showed kindness to the orphaned Prophet when he needed it most.
output.narrative_text: When Abñ Ábil passed away, the Prophet was very sad. Abñ Ábil had taken the Prophet in and treated him like his own son, giving him extra kindness out of sympathy for the bereaved child.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, Abñ Ábil shown with full face, gentle expression. Warm emotional scene, appropriate for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Interior of a modest 7th century Makkah mud-brick home. A man in white thobe and brown cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing near a doorway with warm golden afternoon sunlight streaming behind him. Inside, a dark-skinned older Arab man with thick grey-streaked beard and off-white thobe and black cloak, sitting on a woven mat looking down, one hand on his chest. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
